demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Replace field injection with constructor injection for CustomUserDetailsService.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.why_exists: While field injection still works, constructor injection is the standard for Spring Boot 3.x and required for proper bean lifecycle management in modern configurations; however, strictly speaking, this is often a polishment unless the field was final or required by a specific framework constraint. Given the context of a major migration, this is often part of the 'breaking' structural change to remove the adapter, but technically field injection compiles. I will classify as polishment as it's a best-practice shift, not a compile failure.

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Refactor login endpoint to use a dedicated LoginRequest DTO instead of the User entity for input.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.before: public ResponseEntity<?> createAuthenticationToken(@RequestBody User user) throws Exception

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Update authentication logic to retrieve the authenticated user from SecurityContext and fetch full details from repository.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.after: Authentication authentication = authenticationManager.authenticate(...); SecurityContextHolder.getContext().setAuthentication(authentication); UserDetails userDetails = (UserDetails) authentication.getPrincipal(); User user = userRepository.findByEmail(userDetails.getUsername());

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Update JWT generation call to match the new method signature requiring only the User entity.
Expand the login response payload to include user details (userId, email, name) alongside the token.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.before: Map<String, String> response = new HashMap<>(); response.put("token", jwt);
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.after: Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>(); response.put("token", jwt); response.put("userId", user.getId()); response.put("email", user.getEmail()); response.put("firstName", user.getFirstName()); response.put("lastName", user.getLastName());

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Replace generic exception throwing with specific HTTP status codes for authentication failures.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.before: catch (Exception e) { throw new Exception("Incorrect username or password", e); }
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.after: catch (AuthenticationException e) { return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED).body("Invalid email or password"); } catch (Exception e) { return ResponseEntity.status(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR).body("An error occurred during login"); }

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/con.general_idea: Refactor GoogleIdTokenVerifier initialization from method-local to constructor-injected field for better performance and Spring lifecycle management.
Update user name extraction to use standard OIDC claims (given_name, family_name) instead of parsing the full name string.
Expand the authentication response payload to include user details (ID, email, name) alongside the JWT token.
Remove stack trace printing from the exception handler to improve production logging hygiene.

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/sec.general_idea: Add firstName and lastName to the JWT claims map to include more user information in the token.
demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/sec.before: // Add any other claims you want

demo1/src/main/java/com/example/demo/sec.general_idea: Refactor JWT builder to use setSubject and explicit claim methods instead of setClaims for better structure.
Simplify issuedAt date creation by using new Date() instead of new Date(System.currentTimeMillis()).
Change JWT signature algorithm from HS256 to HS512 for stronger security.

frontend/src/components/Dashboard.jsx.general_idea: Add fallback to localStorage for user profile fields if they are missing from the decoded JWT token.
Clear user profile data from localStorage during logout to ensure a clean state.

frontend/src/components/Login.jsx.general_idea: Update login success handler to persist additional user profile fields (userId, email, firstName, lastName) in localStorage.
Improve error handling in login submission to display a user-friendly alert and log specific error details.
frontend/src/components/Login.jsx.after:
console.error('Login failed', error.response ? error.response.data : error.message);
alert('Login failed. Please check your credentials.');

frontend/src/components/Login.jsx.general_idea: Update Google login success handler to persist additional user profile fields in localStorage.
Remove debug console.log statements from the Google login flow.
